features.summary: Costco reported strong Q4 2021 results with net income of $1.67 billion ($3.76 per share) versus $1.39 billion ($3.13 per share) in the prior year. Net sales increased 17.5% to $61.44 billion, with comparable sales up 15.5% reported (9.4% excluding gas inflation and FX). Membership fee income grew 11.7% to $1.234 billion, with renewal rates improving to 91.3% in US/Canada and 88.7% worldwide. The company opened 20 net new warehouses in fiscal 2021 and plans at least 25 net new units in fiscal 2022.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong performance despite supply chain challenges, with plans to open at least 25 new warehouses in fiscal 2022 including expansion into China, France, and New Zealand. They anticipate maintaining their effective tax rate in the 26-27% range and capital expenditures with "a three in front of it" (over $3 billion). The company has chartered three ocean vessels and leased containers to mitigate shipping delays, while continuing to invest in e-commerce capabilities and Costco Logistics expansion.
features.risks: Costco faces significant inflationary pressures with overall price inflation estimated at 3.5-4.5%, resulting in their first LIFO charge ($30 million) since 2014. Supply chain disruptions include port delays, container shortages, driver shortages, and extended lead times on furniture (16-18 weeks vs. 8-12 weeks previously). The Delta variant has impacted travel bookings, and the company is implementing purchase limits on high-demand items like cleaning supplies and paper products due to supply constraints.
features.key_drivers: Strong membership growth with paid households reaching 61.7 million and executive membership penetration increasing to 25.6 million drives recurring revenue. E-commerce sales grew 8.9% (excluding FX) on top of 91% growth in the prior year, with Costco Logistics representing 24% of US e-commerce sales versus 11% last year. The company's ability to maintain inventory levels and competitive pricing during inflationary periods, combined with market share gains from competitors, supports continued sales momentum and customer loyalty.
